title: Alien Dictionary
description: Given a list of words sorted lexicographically by the rules of an alien language, return a string representing the order of letters in that language. If there is no valid ordering, return an empty string.

examples.explanation: The valid order is 'w', 'e', 'r', 't', 'f'. The words imply 'w' comes before 'e', 'e' before 'r', and so on.
The order is straightforward as 'z' comes before 'x' based on the input.
This is an invalid ordering since 'abc' cannot come before 'ab'. Hence, the output is an empty string.

constraints: The input list will have at most 100 words.
Each word will have a length of at most 100 characters.
All characters in a word are lowercase letters.
hints: Consider using a graph to represent character precedence.
Perform a topological sort on the characters based on the graph.
Look for cycles in the graph to determine if a valid order exists.
An alternative approach could be to use a priority queue for sorting characters.
